Fremont County Emergency Management reports tree down on powerlines near the intersection of S Beaver Springs Rd and Spruce Cir. Fall River Rural Electric Cooperative reports 71 customers out in the area. Time estimated based on fire department dispatch and radar data.
Fall River Rural Electric Cooperative reports 77 customers without power on the US-20 side of Macks Inn. Time estimated based on radar.

Tree estimated at almost 1 foot in diameter down across Cascade Ave between Morrison St and Luke Ln in Macks Inn. Additional small or dead trees down along S Big Springs Loop. Time estimated based on radar and surrounding reports.
1 large tree and 2 smaller trees reported down across a driveway along Old Highway 191. Time estimated based on radar.
Large tree reported down at Elk Creek Estates. Time estimated based on radar.
Fall River Rural Electric Cooperative reports 15 customers without power in Big Springs. Time estimated based on radar.
Report of a vehicle trapped due to a downed tree across the road. Time estimated.
AWOS station KDIJ Driggs-Reed Memorial Airport.

Idaho Transportation Department reports ID-33 is blocked in both directions at milepost 141 due to multiple power lines down. Time estimated based on radar, surrounding reports, and measured wind gusts.

3 power poles damaged, one of them snapped off approximately half way up, and multiple large trees down. Survey team believes the pole snapped due to trees falling on the lines, not due to the strength of the wind itself. Limited details on the large trees due to poor access into the area. Time estimated based on radar and surrounding reports.
Wood and sheet metal horse shed with ground anchors 4 feet deep pulled out of the ground in the area of N 4500 W and 6000 N. Photo and report provided via social media. Time estimated.
Tree estimated at 1.5 feet in diameter snapped off at the base on Goldshire Dr. Time estimated.
Tree limb down on a pickup truck with minimal damage along N 5th St. Time estimated.
Ruby Carson Park littered with large and small downed tree limbs. Photos provided via social media. Time estimated.

7 trees down in the Valley Centre Dr neighborhood, mostly quaking aspens, all snapped off at the roots at or just below ground level. One tree estimated at approximately 2 feet in diameter, the others around 1 foot in diameter. Some trees exhibited some trunk or root rot, others did not. One tree briefly blocked Valley Centre Dr at ID-33, but was quickly cleared. No damage to buildings or vehicles was observed. Time estimated.
Tree limbs down.
Small tree snapped off at ground level at the entrance to Cedron Cemetary. Time estimated based on radar and surrounding reports.
Roof shingles peeled up on home near the intersection of N 3000 W and W 4000 N. Tree litter and small limbs down across the area. Time estimated based on radar and surrounding reports.
Power pole damaged and leaning along E 4000 N. Time estimated based on radar and surrounding reports. Tree litter and small limbs down across the area.
Small portion of sheet metal roof peeled off The Wardrobe business along ID-33. Photo provided via social media. Time estimated.
Fall River Rural Electric Cooperative reports 26 customers without power in downtown Driggs. Measured wind gust to 58 MPH just north at Driggs-Reed Memorial Airport.
Small tree down on a pickup truck off of Clubmoss Ln. Photo provided via social media. Time estimated. Other small branches down across the area.
3 large trees, one cottonwood and two aspens, blown down in driveway along N 1000 E south of E 7000 N. One tree was 16 inches in diameter. Photos provided via social media. Time estimated.
Large tree at least 2 feet in diameter down along Ashley Ave between N 1st St and ID-33. Photo provided by the public. Time estimated.
Pine tree uprooted and down on a trailer home. Damage appears to be very minimal per social media photo. Time estimated.
Large tree at least 2 feet in diameter down along Ashley Ave between N 1st St and ID-33. Photo provided by the public. Time estimated.

Very strong winds reported in Tetonia. 4 foot by 4 foot tarp ripped in half. Tree limb down. Phone line taken off residence. Greenhouse damaged at Tetonia Elementary. Time estimated based on time of report, radar, and surrounding reports.
Very strong winds reported in Tetonia. 4 foot by 4 foot tarp ripped in half. Tree limb down. Phone line taken off residence. Time estimated based on time of report, radar, and surrounding reports.
Tents damaged and destroyed at the craft fair in Victor City Park. One tent weighted down by 120lbs went airborne. Small tree limbs down across the area. Nearby recorded wind gust to 50 MPH.
Numerous large trees snapped off at various heights along Willowbrook Dr. Photos submitted via social media. Time estimated.

Large pine tree estimated at 2 feet in diameter snapped off less than half way up from the ground near the intersection of Ski Hill Rd and Mountain Vista Dr. Photo provided via social media. Two additional trees down blocking Ski Hill Rd at the same location, being cleared with a back hoe. Time estimated.
Tree down at the intersection of ID-33 and Valley Centre Dr. Road has now been cleared. Photo provided by the public via social media. Time estimated.

Visibility between one quarter and one half mile from 200 AM to 315 AM with sustained winds of 40 MPH gusting as high as 57 MPH during this period with falling snow. Temperature dropped from freezing at 145 AM to several degrees below freezing with the squall. LSR time based on lowest observed visibility.
